title: Basics: tighten smoke-local runtime budget and log noise
description: The new smoke-local path works and exits successfully, but it still emits a lot of runtime/artifact noise and can spend longer in the live execution loop than an ideal smoke budget. Follow up by adding an earlier smoke-specific stop condition and quieter publisher/runtime log defaults so local and opt-in CI smoke runs stay fast and readable.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Addressed the biggest smoke-local/runtime-host correctness problems: smoke-local now requires a completed generation instead of any generation start, honors full harness configs for local live-trial use, and the local in-process HiveMind worker inventory no longer goes stale after ~10s. The command is still noisy and longer than ideal, so keeping demos-dql open for additional budget/log tightening is appropriate.

title: Basics: improve AND convergence and local-live trial defaults
description: The live-trial engine and local in-process runtime path now run without the previous stale-worker collapse, preserve last good metrics, and fail fast on all-failure generations, but the AND task still does not reliably meet its stability target in local-live automation. Follow up by tuning seed/template defaults, trial sizing, and/or reproduction selection so the local-live harness reaches a decent AND solution more consistently.
close_reason: Wired the convergence controls into real reproduction behavior and fixed the local-live harness/runtime-host defaults that were causing assignment-timeout and stale-worker collapse; local-live trials now advance through evaluated generations under the in-process stack.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Completed the AND/local-live convergence follow-up in Basics. The seed variation band now actually shapes reproduction limits and mutation probabilities, the live-trial harness now treats execution-session timeout cancellations as timeouts instead of generic stops so it reduces runtime pressure correctly, Connect is routed through the permanent runtime receiver so IO lifecycle broadcasts can target the long-lived client actor, and the in-process local runtime host uses realistic worker-inventory and placement timeout budgets so local-live trials no longer collapse into spawn_assignment_timeout/stale-worker failures. Local-live runs now advance through evaluated generations instead of failing immediately at generation 1.

title: Basics: local stack bootstrap for live harness smoke runs
description: The Basics live harness now supports repeatable headless trial runs and simple auto-tuning, but it still assumes a compatible NBN stack is already running. Follow up with a one-command local bootstrap/smoke path that starts the required runtime services, waits for IO readiness, runs the harness config, and tears the stack down cleanly for developer validation or opt-in CI.
close_reason: The Basics harness now has a one-command local bootstrap and smoke path with temporary stack startup, readiness, execution, and teardown.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Implemented a one-command local smoke path on the existing Basics harness entrypoint. smoke-local now boots a temporary in-process HiveMind/IO/Reproduction/Speciation/Worker stack, rewrites the harness to target that local IO endpoint, runs a reduced smoke profile, writes a report, and tears the stack down. README and harness project wiring were updated accordingly.

title: Basics: bootstrap first external-world demo
description: Track the first real `Basics` demo in this repo. Scope includes the shared environment/orchestration contract, the small operator UI, and one task-plugin issue per initial `2 -> 1` Basics task.
acceptance_criteria:
- The `Basics` environment, UI, and task-plugin work is represented by child issues in a usable implementation order.
- The issue tree keeps `Basics` aligned with supported External World contracts rather than ad hoc runtime internals.
- Resource-sizing and runtime-gap decisions are explicit before coding starts.

title: Basics: create environment and orchestration contract
description: Create the shared `Basics` environment/orchestration contract that every first-wave task plugin will use. This includes the random initial population shape, reproduction/speciation settings, deterministic scoring interface, metrics contract, and the capacity-aware defaults for initial brain count, reproduction run count, and maximum concurrent brains.
design: Use supported IO/repro/speciation surfaces (Connect, GetPlacementWorkerInventory, SpawnBrainViaIO, BrainInfoRequest, output subscriptions, reproduction wrappers, speciation wrappers). The shared environment is template-seeded rather than unconstrained-random, and IO now provides placement-filtered worker capacity for sizing.
acceptance_criteria:
- Define the `Basics` environment scaffold and standardized task-plugin interface under `Basics` for brains with exactly `input_width=2` and `output_width=1`.
- Validate spawned brains through `BrainInfo` before scoring and reject any brain that does not present `2/1` geometry.
- Random-brain constraints and default reproduction settings preserve IO invariants: input region `0`, output region `31`, no axon targets to region `0`, no output-region self-targeting, and default `protect_io_region_neuron_counts` behavior.
- Document or implement the system-capacity sizing strategy for population size, reproduction `run_count`, and max concurrent brains, including manual overrides and deterministic fallback behavior.
- Define the shared metrics contract consumed by the UI: accuracy/fitness over time, best individual, aggregate population stats, and reproduction throughput/capacity summaries.
notes: Implemented Basics solution/library/tests/README plus the runtime IO wrapper, docs, and regression tests for IO-only worker-capacity queries.
close_reason: Implemented the shared Basics environment library, tests, README, template-seeded population contract, parent-selection/run-allocation policy, and the IO-only placement-worker capacity query with runtime docs/tests.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Seed populations are now explicitly template-anchored families with bounded divergence bands, plus shared parent-selection and run-allocation policy so progress pressure, diversity pressure, and resource bounds are handled in the environment instead of per-task plugins.

title: Basics task plugin: One-hot classifier
description: Investigate and, if viable, implement a `One-hot classifier` task plugin for the `Basics` environment. The current `Basics` scope is still `2 -> 1`, so this issue should first resolve whether a one-hot-style framing fits that contract or should be reframed/deferred.
acceptance_criteria:
- Decide whether a one-hot-style classifier is actually compatible with the `Basics` `2 -> 1` environment without hidden channels or undocumented assumptions.
- If it is viable, define and document the canonical dataset, encoding, output interpretation, and deterministic scoring contract.
- If it is not viable, document the mismatch clearly and propose a replacement or reframing that still fits the `Basics` curriculum.
- Emit or specify the standardized score breakdown expected by the shared environment and UI.

title: Basics: execution loop and Start/Stop controls
description: Implement the actual Basics runtime execution loop behind the UI so the operator can start, stop, and monitor an evolving task run instead of only configuring plans. Scope includes spawning the template-seeded initial population, evaluating selected task plugins against running brains, selecting parents, allocating run counts within capacity bounds, reproduction/speciation orchestration, and streaming live metrics back into the UI.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add Start/Stop controls that drive a real execution loop rather than configuration-only preflight.
- Spawn and manage the template-anchored initial population within the current seed-shape and divergence bounds.
- Evaluate the selected Basics task plugin against running brains and feed standardized metric samples back to the UI.
- Select parents and allocate reproduction run counts using the shared scheduling policy while staying within capacity-derived bounds.
- Update live UI metrics/charts during execution and surface clear runtime failure states.
notes: Starting an AND-scoped first pass. Current concrete gap is seed-template publication/generation; execution loop work will be built around the implemented AND plugin and explicit template artifact handling.
close_reason: Implemented the AND-first execution loop with Start/Stop, template publication, live evaluation, reproduction/speciation orchestration, and UI metrics updates.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Implemented the AND-first execution loop. Start/Stop now drive a real session that publishes a 2->1 template artifact when needed, seeds a template-anchored artifact pool, evaluates live spawned brains through the AND plugin, breeds/speciates via IO-only reproduction/speciation calls, updates live UI metrics/charts, and stops cleanly through the new IO kill wrapper in ../NBNv2.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Fixed the local remoting teardown regression in BasicsRuntimeClient. Failed IO connects now fully shut down Proto.Remote before disposing the actor system, so retrying Connect no longer leaves the configured bind port stuck in use. Added a regression test that starts, disposes, and immediately reuses the same local port.

title: Basics: execution-loop hardening and broader task rollout
description: Track post-x3j.11 hardening for the Basics execution loop: stronger task-agnostic evaluation coverage, richer batching/utilization policies, and UI/runtime regression tests as additional task plugins land.
close_reason: Completed the AND-first execution-loop hardening pass: live trials and local in-process runs now preserve last good metrics, classify timeout pressure correctly, route lifecycle broadcasts to the long-lived client actor, honor real variation-band controls in reproduction, and use local runtime-host defaults that avoid the earlier stale-worker and assignment-timeout collapse. Remaining work is now tracked by the individual task-plugin issues and demos-dql.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Hardened the execution loop after user testing: template generation now sorts per-neuron axon lists before serialization, each run starts a fresh speciation epoch before seeding, AND fitness now includes smoother target-proximity scoring and extra near-zero breakdown metrics, and the UI defaults now seed the requested 256/8/128 population settings plus the updated diversity/exploration/run-allocation defaults.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Added selectable output observation modes to Basics. The UI now offers OutputEvent, continuous potential, and continuous buffer; the execution loop defaults to continuous potential for deterministic scoring, supports sparse OutputEvent evaluation as an explicit option, and switches vector source through a new IO-level runtime setter. Residual limitation is tracked separately in demos-x3j.13 because the vector source setter is runtime-global today, not per-brain.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Addressed three user-facing problems: seed-shape boxes now show concrete default/current placeholders instead of 'template'; speciation startup now verifies that the epoch actually advanced and no longer hides failed assignment decisions behind local species fallbacks; and execution snapshots now surface epoch/failure diagnostics while sample observation retries missing outputs before failing a member. Remaining live-runtime automation work is tracked in demos-x3j.14.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Hardened the Basics execution/runtime loop around live trials and local smoke: execution now preserves the last good metrics on cancel/failure instead of resetting to zero, full-generation evaluation failures stop the run immediately instead of spinning through zero-fitness generations, the runtime client consumes IO BrainTerminated events for teardown awareness, and smoke-local can now honor full harness configs while the in-process local runtime no longer ages out its only worker after ~10s due the stale worker-inventory window. Residual convergence work is tracked separately in demos-n4a.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Closed demos-n4a after wiring variation-band controls into reproduction, reclassifying timeout-driven stopped runs correctly in the live harness, routing Connect through the permanent runtime receiver for lifecycle broadcasts, and relaxing the local in-process placement/inventory timeouts so local-live trials can actually progress across generations.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Follow-up polish after close: the real UI run was still failing in generation 1 because potential-mode brains were redundantly issuing per-brain output-vector-source writes and swallowing request failures into 'spawn_failed:unknown'. The execution path now skips the setter when BrainInfo already reports the requested vector source, runtime-client request failures return explicit synthetic failure codes instead of null/unknown, and the UI no longer pre-populates aggressive active sizing overrides (256/8/128) so capacity-driven settings remain the default until the operator explicitly overrides them.

title: Basics: per-run output source isolation
description: Track the remaining limitation after output-mode selection landed: vector source switching currently changes the runtime-wide output-vector policy through IO, which is good enough for a single demo session but not isolated per run/brain. Follow-up scope is per-run or per-brain isolation, clearer UI surfacing of the current runtime-wide effect, and any runtime contract changes needed to remove the shared global side effect.
close_reason: Per-brain output vector source isolation is implemented, documented, and verified across NBNv2 and Basics.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Implemented per-brain output vector source isolation for Basics. IO and control SetOutputVectorSource now accept optional brain_id, HiveMind tracks per-brain output vector source plus explicit overrides, global changes now only retarget brains still following the runtime default, and Basics applies vector mode per spawned evaluation brain. Docs and regression coverage were updated, including a HiveMind test proving a brain-scoped override survives a later global default change while another brain follows the new default.

title: Basics: automated live trial harness
description: Track a deeper live-runtime feedback loop for Basics: repeatable automated trial runs against a running NBN stack, richer per-generation instrumentation, and a path for agent-driven tuning until the demo achieves stable and decent real runtime results instead of only fake-runtime confidence.
close_reason: Live trial harness, structured telemetry, and basic auto-tuning are implemented for Basics and verified in the demo repo.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Implemented a headless Basics live harness in src/Basics.Harness plus reusable live-trial orchestration in Basics.Environment. The harness now repeats real IO-backed trials against a running stack, emits structured per-generation snapshot telemetry into JSON reports, and can apply simple follow-up tuning between trials. Added focused harness tests and README coverage, plus ignored artifact output under Basics/artifacts/.

title: Basics: validate live IO-connected runs and retune defaults
description: Track the remaining work after the harness-side connect retry fixes: rerun Basics against a fresh real IO-connected stack, confirm reliable generation-1 evaluation, gather at least generation-5 progress evidence, and update operator defaults/bounds from that evidence.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Current status before the next live-stack pass: the original generation-1 failure path no longer reproduces in the same way; real live runs now reach nonzero generation-1 evaluations, and the harness has been hardened so connect-window expiry returns ConnectFailed instead of crashing. Remaining work depends on a fresh live IO stack to validate repeated connects, generation progression through >=5, and any resulting default changes.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Real AND live-stack findings after the spawn/setup fixes: conservative profiles now connect and execute reliably on the live IO stack. Verified runs include potential-mode 8/2/1 and exact-template 1/1/1, plus buffer-mode exact-template 1/1/1; the exact-template profiles show generation-over-generation improvement from gen1 accuracy=0.75 fitness=0.764 to gen2 accuracy=1 fitness=1. Additional demo-side fixes landed for setup spawn throttling, longer teardown waits, richer spawn failure diagnostics, and preserving terminal success snapshots when a long-running trial hits its wall-clock timeout during cleanup. Moderate 16/2/4 potential-mode runs still consume the full 240s budget and are therefore a defaults/efficiency question rather than a basic correctness blocker for AND.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Explicit WorkerNode experiments on the live stack: starting WorkerNodes directly from the CLI works and the runtime can discover multiple workers on one machine when they use distinct advertised ports/actor names. A two-worker XOR probe did increase plan inventory to EligibleWorkerCount=2, but under the tested 4/1/2 profile it still failed in generation 1 with spawn request timeouts and missing BrainInfo responses instead of showing a clear throughput win. Current conclusion: worker-launch support in Basics may still be useful later, but it is not yet justified as an immediate UI responsibility based on the observed live-task results.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Task-specific defaults should auto-apply once we have evidence for them. For Basics UI, the desired behavior is: when the selected task changes, apply the current sane default seed-shape/reproduction/sizing profile for that task automatically (until the operator explicitly overrides). XOR is the current forcing function for this work because the live 1-worker exact-template profile reaches 5+ generations but plateaus around 0.5 accuracy / 0.528 fitness instead of steadily improving toward 100%.

title: Basics UI: stop targets and winning-brain export
description: Add target accuracy/fitness stop criteria to the Basics UI, keep the winning brain active when the stop condition is met, and allow exporting the winning definition artifact (.nbn). When runtime/plasticity state is available, extend the same flow to export snapshot/overlay state (.nbs).
close_reason: Implemented stop-target controls, simplicity-biased winner retention, and winning-artifact export in Basics UI.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Winner selection should be deterministic and simplicity-biased: when multiple brains meet the stop target, pick the one with the simplest structure first (starting with neuron/axon count, then other structural simplicity metrics as needed), and only fall back to random selection if the candidates remain tied after all defined simplicity checks.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Implemented Basics UI stop targets and winning-artifact export. The session now retains the simplest winner that meets the configured accuracy/fitness threshold, exposes definition/snapshot artifact refs for export, and the UI can export .nbn plus .nbs when runtime state is available. Verified with 'dotnet build Basics/Basics.sln -c Release --no-restore' and 'dotnet test Basics/Basics.sln -c Release --no-build'.

title: Basics: broaden execution and harness coverage for non-AND task plugins
description: The new OR/XOR/GT/Multiplication plugins have direct plugin and registry coverage, but the execution-session and live-harness tests remain AND-centric. Add at least one non-AND task through the fake-runtime execution path and assert the shared score breakdown propagates through runtime snapshots and harness reporting.
acceptance_criteria: - BasicsExecutionSessionTests exercises at least one non-AND plugin through the fake runtime path.\n- BasicsLiveTrialHarnessTests or adjacent coverage asserts shared score keys survive snapshot/report plumbing for non-AND tasks.\n- Failure-path reporting remains consistent with the shared score breakdown contract.
close_reason: Added non-AND execution/harness coverage and passing Basics solution tests.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Added non-AND environment coverage in BasicsExecutionSessionTests and BasicsLiveTrialHarnessTests. The fake runtime can now emit OR/XOR/GT/Multiplication semantics for test routing, execution-session coverage now runs OR through the real session loop, and harness coverage now asserts OR score-breakdown keys survive snapshot/report plumbing. Verification: dotnet test Basics.sln -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp.

title: Basics: create configuration and metrics UI
description: Create the small `Basics` UI for connection/configuration, capacity-derived defaults, runtime overrides, and metrics/plotting. This should follow the environment contract rather than inventing its own task or population model.
design: Use IO GetPlacementWorkerInventory for capacity discovery. SettingsMonitor fields become optional/operator-only for workflows outside runtime sizing, rather than mandatory for the Basics environment.
acceptance_criteria:
- The UI captures the IO endpoint, with additional service endpoints optional for operator workflows rather than required for capacity discovery.
- After connection, the operator can fetch current system capacity through IO and apply suggested defaults for initial population size, reproduction `run_count`, and max concurrent brains.
- The operator can override auto-sized settings and edit the core environment/task parameters without bypassing validation.
- The UI exposes task selection, relevant reproduction/speciation settings, and clear connection/runtime error states.
- The UI plots or otherwise surfaces accuracy/fitness over time plus best-individual and population-level metrics defined by the shared environment contract.
notes: Started implementing the Basics UI project on top of the shared environment contract and the new IO capacity query.
close_reason: Implemented the Basics UI shell and configuration workflow on top of the shared environment contract, including IO-only capacity discovery and live-ready metric/chart surfaces.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Implemented a new Avalonia Basics UI project with IO-only capacity fetch, task selection, template-family configuration, bounded divergence controls, reproduction/speciation scheduling controls, override application, validation/error surfacing, and chart/metric panels that are ready for live samples once task plugins begin publishing data.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Fixed a post-close UI regression: the default IO address is now 127.0.0.1:12050, and AsyncRelayCommand/RelayCommand now marshal CanExecuteChanged notifications onto Avalonia's UI thread so Connect no longer throws an invalid-thread exception.

title: Basics task plugin: AND
description: Implement the `AND` task plugin for the `Basics` environment. The plugin should evaluate a `2 -> 1` brain against the full deterministic truth table and return standardized fitness/accuracy outputs to the shared environment/UI.
acceptance_criteria:
- Define and document the canonical input/output encoding for `AND` within the `Basics` environment.
- Score the full deterministic truth table using the shared `2 -> 1` plugin contract.
- Validate finite inputs, expected output width, and tick-aligned scoring before awarding fitness.
- Emit standardized score breakdown data consumable by the shared environment and UI.
notes: Started implementing the first Basics task plugin and wiring the shared task surface so the UI can recognize at least one available task.
close_reason: Implemented the AND Basics task plugin, tests, registry wiring, environment task-selection contract, and UI task availability integration.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Implemented a new Basics.Tasks project with an AND plugin, deterministic truth-table dataset, explicit geometry/tick-alignment/finite-value validation, standardized score breakdown output, and registry wiring so the Basics UI now resolves AND as an available task.

title: Basics task plugin: OR
description: Implement the `OR` task plugin for the `Basics` environment. The plugin should evaluate a `2 -> 1` brain against the full deterministic truth table and return standardized fitness/accuracy outputs to the shared environment/UI.
acceptance_criteria:
- Define and document the canonical input/output encoding for `OR` within the `Basics` environment.
- Score the full deterministic truth table using the shared `2 -> 1` plugin contract.
- Validate finite inputs, expected output width, and tick-aligned scoring before awarding fitness.
- Emit standardized score breakdown data consumable by the shared environment and UI.
close_reason: Implemented OR task plugin, docs, registry wiring, and passing Basics solution tests.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Implemented the OR Basics task plugin with canonical 0/1 truth-table encoding, shared deterministic scalar scoring, registry wiring, README documentation, and direct task/registry regression tests. Verification: dotnet test Basics.sln -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp.

title: Basics task plugin: XOR
description: Implement the `XOR` task plugin for the `Basics` environment. The plugin should evaluate a `2 -> 1` brain against the full deterministic truth table and return standardized fitness/accuracy outputs to the shared environment/UI.
acceptance_criteria:
- Define and document the canonical input/output encoding for `XOR` within the `Basics` environment.
- Score the full deterministic truth table using the shared `2 -> 1` plugin contract.
- Validate finite inputs, expected output width, and tick-aligned scoring before awarding fitness.
- Emit standardized score breakdown data consumable by the shared environment and UI.
close_reason: Implemented XOR task plugin, docs, registry wiring, and passing Basics solution tests.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Implemented the XOR Basics task plugin with canonical 0/1 truth-table encoding, shared deterministic scalar scoring, registry wiring, README documentation, and direct task/registry regression tests. Verification: dotnet test Basics.sln -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp.

title: Basics task plugin: GT
description: Implement the `GT` (`a > b`) task plugin for the `Basics` environment. The plugin should evaluate a `2 -> 1` brain on a deterministic comparison set and return standardized fitness/accuracy outputs to the shared environment/UI.
acceptance_criteria:
- Define and document the canonical input/output encoding and comparison dataset for `GT` within the `Basics` environment.
- Score the deterministic evaluation set using the shared `2 -> 1` plugin contract.
- Validate finite inputs, expected output width, and tick-aligned scoring before awarding fitness.
- Emit standardized score breakdown data consumable by the shared environment and UI.
close_reason: Implemented GT task plugin, docs, registry wiring, and passing Basics solution tests.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Implemented the GT Basics task plugin with the canonical bounded 3x3 comparison grid in [0,1], shared deterministic scalar scoring, registry wiring, README documentation, and direct task/registry regression tests. Verification: dotnet test Basics.sln -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp.

title: Basics task plugin: Multiplication
description: Implement the `Multiplication` task plugin for the `Basics` environment. The plugin should score a `2 -> 1` brain on a documented bounded input domain and normalized output target, then return standardized fitness outputs.
acceptance_criteria:
- Define and document the canonical input domain, output range/normalization, and sampling strategy for `Multiplication` within the `Basics` environment.
- Score a deterministic evaluation set using the shared `2 -> 1` plugin contract.
- Validate finite inputs, expected output width, and tick-aligned scoring before awarding fitness.
- Emit standardized score breakdown data consumable by the shared environment and UI.
close_reason: Implemented Multiplication task plugin, docs, registry wiring, and passing Basics solution tests.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Implemented the Multiplication Basics task plugin with the canonical bounded 5x5 grid over [0,1], normalized a*b targets, tolerance-based accuracy, shared deterministic scalar scoring, registry wiring, README documentation, and direct task/registry regression tests. Verification: dotnet test Basics.sln -c Release --disable-build-servers --artifacts-path .artifacts-temp.

title: Basics task plugin: Noisy in -> clean out
description: Implement the `Noisy in -> clean out` task plugin for the `Basics` environment. Because this is still a `2 -> 1` task, the issue should make the denoising formulation explicit instead of leaving the role of each input implicit.
acceptance_criteria:
- Define and document the exact `2 -> 1` denoising formulation, including how the noisy signal is encoded and what the clean target represents.
- Score a deterministic evaluation set using the shared plugin contract.
- Validate finite inputs, expected output width, and any required temporal assumptions before awarding fitness.
- Emit standardized score breakdown data consumable by the shared environment and UI.

title: Basics task plugin: Delayed out
description: Implement the `Delayed out` task plugin for the `Basics` environment, where output is expected after a configured number of ticks rather than on the same tick as the triggering input.
acceptance_criteria:
- Define and document the exact delayed-output task contract, including the delay parameter(s), episode structure, and target output timing.
- Score with explicit respect for NBN two-phase tick semantics so expectations align with `N` input / `N+1+delay` visibility rather than same-tick assumptions.
- Validate finite inputs, expected output width, and tick-aligned scoring before awarding fitness.
- Emit standardized score breakdown data consumable by the shared environment and UI.
